content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


September 26, 1989


James W. March, Esq.
AMBase Corporation of New York
59 Maiden Lane
New York, New York 10038

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-89-520

Dear Mr. March:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you on behalf of AmBase's California and Federal Political
Action Committees concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of
the Political Reform Act.

Your letter, dated August 31, 1989, correctly summarizes the
advice I provided to you. A copy of your letter is enclosed for
your convenience.

If you have any additional questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,


Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[illegible]

By: Alice Hughes
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure




428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

55 Meadow Lane
New York NY 10536
212-510-6800

FPPC

SEP 1 9 06 AM '89

FPPC
SEP 01 1999
RECEIVED

AMBASE

August 31, 1989


Ms. Alice Hughes
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Hughes:

     In accordance with our recent conversation, enclosed is my
letter to Cindy Durham of the "Pete Wilson for Governor" campaign
staff along with a copy of the check for the $2,500 contribution to
"Pete Wilson for Governor." Please note that the check was issued
by AmBase's California Registered Political Action Committee
(AmBase Political Action Committee of California). This
contribution was made after we received reimbursement from the
Wilson campaign of the $2,500 contribution made by our Federal
Political Action Committee (AmBase Corporation Political Action
Committee).

     The reimbursement to our Federal PAC and subsequent $2,500
contribution by our California PAC completes the unwinding of a
process that began with the $2,500 contribution made to Pete Wilson
from our Federal PAC in May, 1989. Shortly after this contribution
was made, you informed me that a Federal PAC could contribute only
$1,000 to a candidate for state office in California. As noted in
my correspondence of July 12, and in accordance with our earlier
discussion, we attempted to correct this error by having our
California PAC reimburse our Federal PAC for the $2,500
contribution it made to Pete Wilson.

     In late July, I asked Stephanie Fitzgerald of the Federal
Elections Commission (FEC) how the $2,500 contribution should be
reported in our semi-annual FEC filing. Ms. Fitzgerald informed me
that we should not have reimbursed our Federal PAC since FEC
regulations prohibit transfers from non-federal accounts. Although
Ms. Fitzgerald understood that the transfer was not made to
circumvent the FEC rule, she strongly advised that we transfer the
money back to the California PAC. We followed Ms. Fitzgerald's
advice and on August 9, 1989 our Federal PAC issued a $2,500 check
to our California PAC.

                                        AmBase Corporation of New York

Ms. Alice Hughes
August. 31, 1989
Page 2

     The $2,500 Federal PAC contribution and the $2,500 transfer
from the California PAC to the Federal PAC both took place prior to
July 1, 1989 and were therefore reported in the Form 420
semi-annual filing covering the January 1, 1989 - June 30, 1989
period.  The reimbursement by Wilson of the $2,500 Federal PAC
contribution and the $2,500 contribution by the California PAC to
Wilson were made after July 1, 1989 and will therefore be reported
in the next Form 420 semi-annual statement, to be submitted by
January 31, 1990.  Please advise if we should submit any other
official report of these post July 1, 1989 transactions.

     On behalf of the AmBase Corporation, I thank you for your
patience and understanding.  Your assistance has been invaluable.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        James W. March, Esq.

JWM:1r

cc:  Carol McCluskey - 27
     Robert Thomson - 27

AMBASE

August 31, 1989


Ms. Cindy Durham
Pete Wilson for Governor
2551 San Diego Avenue
Suite B200
San Diego, CA 92110

Re: Political Contribution

Dear Ms. Durham:

We have received the reimbursement of the $2,500 contribution
made by our Federal Political Action Committee (AmBase Corporation
Political Action Committee) and enclose a $2,500 contribution made
by our California Political Action Committee (AmBase Political
Action Committee of California).

On behalf of AmBase Corporation, I apologize for any
inconvenience we may have caused and thank you for your patience
and understanding.

Sincerely,

James W. March

Sincerely,

|||00200||" !!0 2!000008 9!: 0409 2!EB||"

September 6, 1989


James W. March
AmBase Corporation of New York
59 Maiden Lane
New York, NY  10038

                                        Re:  Letter No. 89-520

Dear Mr. March:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on September 1, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Chief Technical Assistance
                                            and Analysis Division

sections.question_synthetic: AmBase Corporation sought confirmation of telephone advice regarding proper reporting and contribution limits under California's Political Reform Act for its federal and state political action committees' contributions to the Pete Wilson for Governor campaign.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that the requestor's letter accurately summarized the telephone advice previously provided regarding campaign disclosure provisions and the correction process for the over-limit federal PAC contribution.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter (Aug 31) and FPPC response letter (Sept 26), plus related correspondence. No explicit QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections in formal format; advice was initially provided by telephone and confirmed in writing. Confidence reduced due to informal nature of initial advice and multiple supporting documents rather than single formatted letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: AmBase Corporation sought confirmation of telephone advice regarding proper reporting and contribution limits under California's Political Reform Act for its federal and state political action committees' contributions to the Pete Wilson for Governor campaign.

CONCLUSION: Your letter, dated August 31, 1989, correctly summarizes the advice I provided to you.

embedding.summary: This file documents the FPPC's confirmation of telephone advice to AmBase Corporation regarding contribution limits and reporting requirements after the requestor's federal PAC inadvertently contributed $2,500 (exceeding the $1,000 limit for federal PACs to California state candidates) and subsequently corrected the error through reimbursement and a compliant contribution from its California PAC.
